title: Searsia nitida
taxonomy: Searsia nitida is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It is native to southern Africa. Searsia nitida is a member of the genus Searsia, which was previously classified under the name Rhus. The family Anacardiaceae is a group of plants commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family. Searsia nitida's classification is further grouped under the order Sapindales.
uses: Searsia nitida, also known as the black thorn or kei apple, has been traditionally used in various folk remedies. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and bark are used to treat fever and digestive issues. The berries are edible and rich in antioxidants. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal efficacy and potential side effects. Searsia nitida's medicinal uses may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.

parts: Searsia nitida, also known as the shiny bukkehorn, is a plant species used in traditional medicine. The leaves and bark of Searsia nitida are said to have medicinal properties.
